$description: Per-scenario perceptual-hash tolerance for the visual-regression loop. Values are Hamming-distance thresholds on a 3×3 grid pHash; pairs with max-tile distance > threshold count as drift. Default applied when a scenario id is absent from this map.
$rules: Bumping a threshold to silence drift is a code smell. Prefer to fix the regression, or bless the new screenshot via `bash scripts/loop/run_one_scenario_loop.sh --bless <scenario.json>`.
Per-step overrides (`<scenario_id>.<step_idx>`) take precedence over the per-scenario value.

Threshold semantics (post-grid-pHash): 0 = identical, 64 = totally different per tile. Empirical baseline for clean runs is 0 across all tiles. The 3×3 grid takes max-tile distance, so a single drifted region trips drift.

skip_diff is for scenarios whose visual output is intrinsically non-deterministic (multi-token tween settling, mid-tween dispatch sequences). They still run and still capture per-step screenshots for the critic agent to read — only the perceptual-hash diff is suppressed.

$skip_diff_rationale.horde_movement: 20 Boyz models move toward a target with congestion blocking. Empirically run-to-run drift is hd=32 max-tile even with rng_seed=42 — the per-model tween destinations depend on continuous physics interactions that don't quantize to identical pixels. The scenario validates engine state (models reach the target despite congestion), not visual fidelity.
$skip_diff_rationale.co_offer_after_charge: Four dispatch_actions fire in rapid succession (SELECT_FIGHTER, DECLINE_EPIC_CHALLENGE, PILE_IN, CONSOLIDATE) with no inter-action settle frames. The per-step screenshot after each step captures the engine mid-tween (camera pan, token reposition, dialog opening). Run-to-run drift is hd~20 on different steps each run, so neither blessing nor threshold-bumping gives a stable signal. The scenario validates the counter-offensive trigger fires correctly, asserted via expect_action_result and expect_phase_property — visual diff adds no signal.
